data_in.description: SMRT dataset with 80,038 experimental retention times and molecular structures

data_out.description: Model performance comparison table with mean and median absolute errors for descriptors-only, fingerprints-only, and combined feature models
Trained regression model weights and architecture specification

domain_knowledge: Fingerprints (MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, Path) encode structural patterns as binary or count vectors and typically capture local chemical neighborhoods better than global molecular descriptors for chromatographic retention prediction.

Mean absolute error (MAE) and median absolute error (MdAE) are both essential metrics for RT prediction; MAE is sensitive to outliers while MdAE is robust, allowing complementary assessment of model reliability.

Heavily regularized DNNs with cosine annealing warm restarts and stochastic weight averaging are necessary to prevent overfitting on large molecular feature spaces (5,666 descriptors + 2,214 fingerprints) and improve generalization.
The SMRT dataset contains 80,038 experimental retention times from real liquid chromatography measurements, making it a robust benchmark for comparing feature engineering strategies.

finding: Fingerprints tend to perform better than descriptors alone across the three feature conditions tested (descriptors only, fingerprints only, and both types combined).
inputs: SMRT dataset with 80,038 experimental retention times and molecular structures
methodology_summary: Load SMRT dataset with 80,038 experimental retention times and molecular structures.
Generate 5,666 molecular descriptors and 2,214 fingerprints (MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, Path) using alvaDesc.
Train heavily regularized DNN with cosine annealing warm restarts and stochastic weight averaging on three feature conditions: descriptors-only, fingerprints-only, and combined.
Evaluate each model using mean absolute error (MAE) and median absolute error (MdAE) on held-out test set.

Validation: Confirm that fingerprints-only achieves lower MAE and MdAE than descriptors-only, and comparable or superior performance to combined features, matching reported findings of fingerprint superiority.

task_description: Train and compare three machine learning models (descriptors-only, fingerprints-only, and combined) on the SMRT dataset using alvaDesc-generated features to verify that fingerprints outperform descriptors for retention time prediction.

task_inputs.label: SMRT dataset with experimental retention times

task_objective: Train and compare machine learning regressors using the three feature conditions on METLIN SMRT data and report relative model performance metrics to validate that fingerprints outperform descriptors.

task_outputs.label: Model performance metrics across three feature conditions
Trained DNN model weights and configuration

title: Analyze fingerprints-only vs descriptors-only vs combined feature sets on SMRT retention time prediction
uncertainty_notes: This card was generated by the LLM-assisted pipeline and needs scientific expert review.
Each TracedClaim's evidence_span has been substring-checked against its source section; see logs/llm_calls.jsonl and capsules/<task_id>/quality_report.json for groundedness results.
Synthesis grounding: the following tools/outputs were NOT found in the source paper and are inferred — verify before use: Trained regression model weights and architecture specification.
workflow_description: 1. Load the SMRT dataset containing 80,038 experimental retention times and molecular structures. 2. Generate 5,666 molecular descriptors and 2,214 fingerprints (MACCS166, Extended Connectivity, and Path Fingerprints) using alvaDesc for all molecules. 3. Split data into training and test sets. 4. Train a heavily regularized deep neural network with cosine annealing warm restarts and stochastic weight averaging using only descriptors; record mean absolute error (MAE) and median absolute error (MdAE). 5. Train the same model architecture using only fingerprints; record MAE and MdAE. 6. Train the same model using both descriptors and fingerprints combined; record MAE and MdAE. 7. Compare performance metrics across all three conditions and verify that fingerprints-only achieves lower error than descriptors-only and comparable or better error than the combined approach.
